updateMessage: Adds experimental Germany 2026 personal tax rules, including formula-based income tax, social insurance approximations, and Abgeltungsteuer-style investment wrappers.

incomeTax.name: Einkommensteuer
incomeTax.tooltip: German Einkommensteuer formula tariff for 2026. FinSim applies the statutory tariff directly to the modeled annual income base; German deduction mechanics that are not representable are documented in the gap report.

socialContributions.name: Renten- und Arbeitslosenversicherung
socialContributions.tooltip: Grouped employee contribution bucket for the statutory pension insurance (9.3%) and unemployment insurance (1.3%) using the 2026 annual earnings cap of EUR 101,400.

socialContributions.name: Kranken- und Pflegeversicherung
socialContributions.tooltip: Grouped employee contribution bucket using the 2026 14.6% general health-insurance rate, the official 2.9% average add-on rate, and the 3.6% nursing-care rate. FinSim uses a standard employee share approximation of 10.55% up to EUR 69,750 and does not model childless surcharges, child-count reductions, Saxony-specific splits, or pensioner-specific rules.

capitalGainsTax.name: Kapitalertragsteuer
capitalGainsTax.tooltip: Flat 25% capital-income tax approximation for modeled securities gains. The EUR 1,000 saver allowance is applied as the shared annual exemption pool for realized gains. Church tax, dividends/interest buckets, and full fund-accrual mechanics are documented in the gap report.

helpText.label: Tax-Advantaged Pension
helpText.contributionDescriptionP1:
Enter how much of the modeled German annual pension cap you want to contribute from salary.

FinSim uses a simplified tax-advantaged pension proxy based on the tax-free occupational-pension cap under Section 3 No. 63 EStG.
helpText.contributionDescriptionP2:
Enter how much of your partner's modeled German annual pension cap you want to contribute from salary.

FinSim uses the same simplified German pension proxy for both people.
helpText.statePensionDescriptionP1:
Enter your expected monthly statutory pension amount.

Use your latest Deutsche Rentenversicherung estimate or other pension statement.
helpText.statePensionDescriptionP2:
Enter your partner's expected monthly statutory pension amount.

Use their latest Deutsche Rentenversicherung estimate or other pension statement.

investmentTypes.label: Direktaktien
investmentTypes.helpText: Direct shareholdings are modeled under the flat German capital-income tax rate of ${taxRules.capitalGainsTax.rate,percentage}, with the solidarity surcharge applied through the ruleset adapter. Church tax and dividend-specific withholding details are not modeled.

investmentTypes.label: Aktienfonds / ETFs
investmentTypes.helpText: German equity-fund / ETF gains are approximated using the 30% partial exemption for Aktienfonds, so only 70% of the gain is taxed at the flat 25% capital-income rate before the solidarity surcharge. The annual Vorabpauschale and basis adjustments are not modeled.
